title: What HNSW m and ef_construction control
description: The m (connectivity) and ef_construction parameters set graph quality at build time and trade memory and build speed for recall.
body: Reference for tuning HNSW build parameters. m sets the number of bi-directional edges per node (higher m means better recall and more memory), and ef_construction sets how many candidates are explored while inserting each node (higher means a better graph but slower build). Use when configuring an HNSW index build. Do not confuse these build-time parameters with ef_search, which is set per query; raising ef_search later cannot fix a graph built with too-low m. Validation signals include recall improving with higher m at the cost of index size. Risk boundary: m and ef_construction are baked in at build time, so a too-low choice forces a full rebuild to fix, making them a deliberate up-front decision.
